packageRules.description: One weekly PR for all image digest bumps: a Dockerfile edit changes the CI buildhash and triggers one full rebuild — don't drip-feed that. docker-compose.yaml image pins ride along in the same group and deploy without a rebuild (compose is not a buildhash input).

pnpm majors are held back deliberately: a new major can change install defaults/behavior against upstream auth_portal's lockfile (the v9.0 lockfile FORMAT itself spans pnpm 9-11), which would fail --frozen-lockfile on every frontend build. Bump manually after testing a frontend build with the new major.

node majors must be bumped by hand: the distroless runtime line embeds the major in the repo NAME (nodejs22-debian12), which Renovate cannot bump, so a node:22-slim -> node:24-slim PR would half-bump the pair. Edit both FROM lines in frontend/Dockerfile in one commit.

Gradle lives in two managed places (ARG GRADLE_VERSION in the backend Dockerfile via the custom manager below, and gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties via the built-in gradle-wrapper manager) — one grouped PR keeps the CI image and the local wrapper in lockstep.

customManagers.description: Exact pnpm pin inside the frontend build image
Gradle distribution wget-installed inside the backend build image
GraalVM native build-tools Gradle plugin, sed-injected into build.gradle inside the backend Dockerfile (invisible to the built-in gradle manager)
